t_ch: 又开始了，又开始了。人造的就不是命？铁打的就不是命？那自主觉醒的呢，宇宙自然形成的呢，星神创造的呢？
t_en: Here we go again. Can't the artificial still be a life? Can't metal make a life? What about those that reached enlightenment themselves, that were naturally created by the universe or created by the Aeons?

t_ch: 现有的那些生物分类法，不过是少数学界精英仗着他们对自然浅薄的认知，私自提出的劣等理论罢了…依我看，都是胡扯！
t_en: Existing taxonomies are just shoddy theories clutched onto by a few academic elites desperately relying on their shallow understanding of nature... In my opinion, that's all nonsense!

t_ch: 不想和你辩了。
t_en: I don't want to debate you any further.

t_ch: …哼，我看你是知难而退了吧？靠旁边站点，别影响我表演——我还得靠才艺赚润滑油钱呢。
t_en: ...Yeah, that's right. Back off and retain some dignity. Move aside and don't ruin my performance — I still have to busk to earn my lubricant oil.

t_ch: 说到底，为什么你要故意惹怒一头熊呢？
t_en: Why would you want to deliberately annoy a bear, anyway?

t_ch: 在你校准对方情绪的一瞬间，一名智械女性突然出现在了眼前。
t_en: The moment you calibrate the target's mood, an Intellitron girl appears out of nowhere.

t_ch: 再次警告你，不准歧视任何生物！就算是一只过街的太空鼠，它的生命也和你是平等的！少来和我杠！
t_en: Let me warn you again, do not discriminate against any creature! Even if it's just a space rat crossing the street, its life is just as precious as yours! So stop trying to get smart with me!

t_ch: 喝下这瓶苏乐达，快乐美梦速速达…不对，这又不是上节目。
t_en: Grab a bottle of SoulGlad, make your dreams a blast... Wait no, I'm not live on stream right now.

t_ch: 你是电视里那个…
t_en: Aren't you from that show on TV...

t_ch: 你为什么要唱广告词？
t_en: Why are you singing the advertising jingle?

t_ch: 哎？你听到了？
t_en: Huh? You heard that?

t_ch: 别、别那么大声啊，要是被人发现可不好。嘘……
t_en: D—don't be so loud. It'd be a real shame if other people find out. Shh...

t_ch: 你很出名吗？
t_en: Are you a celebrity?

t_ch: 我叫乌玛，是家族的新闻主持人。出名…倒是算不上。
t_en: My name's Uma, I'm a news anchor from The Family. As for being a celebrity... I can't really say that.

t_ch: 不过，如果你经常关注路边的屏幕，应该多少见过我主持的节目。
t_en: But if you keep an eye out on the roadside billboards, you ought to have come across a few of my shows.

t_ch: 为什么不能被人发现？
t_en: Why don't you want to be found?

t_ch: 咳咳…我叫乌玛，是家族的新闻主持人。如果你经常关注路边屏幕，应该多少见过我主持的节目。
t_en: *Ahem*... My name's Uma, I'm a news anchor from The Family. If you keep an eye out on the roadside billboards, you ought to have come across a few of my shows.

t_ch: 理论上现在是工作时间，不过我没什么活干，就偷着溜出来逛一逛…你可不许告我的状啊。
t_en: Technically, I'm currently clocked in, but I don't have anything to do, so I sneaked out for a stroll... Don't rat me out.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 可以，但不许涉及敏感话题。
t_en: Sure, but no sensitive topics.

t_ch: 你怎么看待那些新闻？
t_en: What do you think of the news?

t_ch: 说实话，我觉得许多新闻都有…创作的成分，但无伤大雅。
t_en: To be honest, I think a lot of news reporting has elements of... creativity in it, but it's harmless.

t_ch: 和报纸一样，电视新闻最重要的是搞清楚什么能说，什么不能。
t_en: Like newspapers, the most important thing about TV news is to figure out what can and can't be said...

t_ch: 所以我们匹诺康尼的媒体人都遵循一个原则：报喜不报忧。
t_en: So us media here in Penacony tend to stick by a common rule: Report feel-good news only.

t_ch: 这未免太不负责了吧？
t_en: Isn't this grossly irresponsible?

t_ch: 唉，我以前也这么觉得——新闻不是应该还原真相吗？可现在我有了更深的理解。
t_en: *sigh* I used to feel the same way — shouldn't the news restore the truth? But I've since come to a deeper understanding.

t_ch: 你想啊，逐梦客们来到匹诺康尼是为了什么？还不是为了体验「美梦」。
t_en: Think about it — why do Dreamchasers make their journey to Penacony if not to experience sweet dreams?

t_ch: 是美梦，就要避免让里面的人被各种丧气事烦恼——你瞧，新闻在这里更像是一种「服务」，它的用处是增加大家的安全感和幸福感。
t_en: So in a sweet dream, people shouldn't be depressed by all kinds of troublesome things — just look, the news here is more like a kind of service with the express purpose of increasing everyone's sense of security and happiness.

t_ch: 既然大家只想体会快乐，那我们只报道那些能带来快乐的东西就够了。
t_en: Seeing as everyone only wants to pursue happiness, we only report on the things that bring people happiness — and that's enough.

t_ch: 不管什么时候都蒸蒸日上，不管什么地方都一片祥和。没有需要战胜的敌人，也没有需要担心的明天。
t_en: Times are prosperous no matter when, and it's peaceful no matter where you are. There are no enemies to fight here, and no tomorrows to ever worry about.

t_ch: 现实中的坏消息已经够多了，犯不着在梦境里再去强调它们，是吧？
t_en: There's already enough bad news out there in the real world, so why go and underscore it in the dreamscape?

t_ch: 听上去很明智。
t_en: Sounds pretty sensible.

t_ch: 对吧对吧？以前我还觉得，新闻不还原真相怎么行。可现在我有了更深的理解。
t_en: Right? Right? I used to think the news had to restore the truth. But I've since come to a deeper understanding.

t_ch: 你想啊，逐梦客们来到匹诺康尼是为了什么？还不是为了体验「美梦」。
t_en: Think about it — why do Dreamchasers make their journey to Penacony if not to experience sweet dreams?

t_ch: 是美梦，就要避免让里面的人被各种丧气事烦恼——你瞧，新闻在这里更像是一种「服务」，它的用处是增加大家的安全感和幸福感。
t_en: So in a sweet dream, people shouldn't be depressed by all kinds of troublesome things — just look, the news here is more like a kind of service with the express purpose of increasing everyone's sense of security and happiness.

t_ch: 既然大家只想体会快乐，那我们只报道那些能带来快乐的东西就够了。
t_en: Seeing as everyone only wants to pursue happiness, we only report on the things that bring people happiness — and that's enough.

t_ch: 不管什么时候都蒸蒸日上，不管什么地方都一片祥和。没有需要战胜的敌人，也没有需要担心的明天。
t_en: Times are prosperous no matter when, and it's peaceful no matter where you are. There are no enemies to fight here, and no tomorrows to ever worry about.

t_ch: 现实中的坏消息已经够多了，犯不着在梦境里再去强调它们，是吧？
t_en: There's already enough bad news out there in the real world, so why go and underscore it in the dreamscape?

t_ch: 你喜欢你的工作吗？
t_en: Do you like your job?

t_ch: 我其实不喜欢待在演播厅。那间屋子的门噪音超大，就像…就像一只濒死的伊须磨洲海豚。
t_en: I don't actually like being in the studio. The door to that room is super noisy, just like... just like a dying Insumousu dolphin.

t_ch: 我不止一次考虑过换份工作。但亲耳听见来宾们谈论匹诺康尼的近况之后，我意识到这份工作意义重大。
t_en: I've considered changing careers more than once. But after hearing guests talk about Penacony's current situation, I came to realize how important this line of work is.

t_ch: 家族正费尽心思为人们编织没有锐角的美梦，我也要贡献自己的一份力量才行。
t_en: The Family does all they can to weave a dreamscape without any sharp corners, and I also want to play my part, too.

t_ch: 没什么。
t_en: Nothing else.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: 喂喂，你该不会是想…做什么失礼的事吧？
t_en: Hey, don't tell me you're trying to... do something rude?

t_ch: 绒球对皮皮西来说可是很私密的，随便乱摸会出现严重后果！
t_en: Fluff balls are very personal things when it comes to the Pepeshi people. There's going to be serious consequences if you just pet them without consent!

t_ch: 据说某皮皮西被摸球之后，连整个人的性别都变了——真是太可怕啦！
t_en: I heard that one Pepeshi ended up changing gender entirely after having their fluff ball stroked — that's scary!

t_ch: 这次先原谅你的冒犯，下不为例。
t_en: I'll look past your transgression this time, but not again.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 再见，愿「黄金的时刻」只有好事发生。
t_en: Goodbye. May only good things happen in the Golden Hour.

t_ch: 乌玛的神情看不出喜悲，正如她播报新闻时的模样。
t_en: Uma's expression betrays no hint of joy nor sorrow; the very likeness of when she's reporting on the news.

t_ch: 是你啊…咱们的谈话可不能公开哦。
t_en: Oh, it's you... Our conversation is off the record.

t_ch: 咳哼……
t_en: *Ahem*...

t_ch: 乌玛清了清嗓子，整理好衣襟，重新恢复到最得体的模样。
t_en: Uma clears her throat, straightens out her clothing, and sets up her most appealing look.

t_ch: 乌玛紧皱着眉，头顶的绒球因愤怒而来回摆动。
t_en: Uma frowns, and the fluff ball on her head darts back and forth in anger.

t_ch: 乌玛皱起了眉，头顶的绒球因愤怒而来回摆动。
t_en: Uma frowns, and the fluff ball on her head darts back and forth in anger.

t_ch: 「匹诺康尼新闻台」，听起来很高大上对不对？要我说，就是个草台班子！
t_en: "Penacony News" sounds pretty prestigious, right? But if you ask me, it's college radio!

t_ch: 一群撰稿人头衔倒是高，什么「约瑟夫新闻奖提名」、「银河百大金牌写手」…实际肚子里墨水少得要死，都快比不上报社的狗仔了。
t_en: Sure, they've got a bunch of fancy titles like "Nominated for the Joseph Journalism Award" and "Top <unbreak>100</unbreak> Writer in the Cosmos"... but in actuality, they're such hacks that they're almost as bad as the paparazzi.

t_ch: 之前有条稿子，居然写起了知更鸟小姐的绯闻来，太离谱了！还好最后没有播出。
t_en: There was a draft they put out a while ago that actually mentioned the Miss Robin scandal. That's outrageous! Luckily it never made it to air.

t_ch: 这些人心里没数的吗？编织美梦本就不易，还非得用针把梦泡戳破才行？
t_en: Are these people really so clueless? Weaving a sweet dream is no easy task, so do you really have to pop that dream bubble with a needle?

t_ch: 就该用蛋糕塞住他们的嘴！
t_en: Should stuff their mouths full of cake instead!

t_ch: 我的理想，只会动摇人们的安稳生活…真讽刺啊。
t_en: My ideals would only shake the stability of people's lives... how ironic.

t_ch: 唉…其实，我当年并没有想过要做一个裱糊匠。
t_en: *sigh* I've never actually thought about being a reality fixer-upper before.

t_ch: 我的梦想是当一名调查记者，寻找宇宙间被掩盖的真相。让大家不再因信息差而受到误导。
t_en: My dream was always to be an investigative reporter, chasing down hidden truths throughout the cosmos so that people would no longer be misled by low-quality information.

t_ch: 可由于「万界之癌」的影响，我只能留在梦境里，帮助家族文过饰非。我一直追求的真相反倒成了最无所谓的东西。
t_en: But thanks to the Cancer of All Worlds, all I can do is remain here in the dreamscape and help The Family smooth over their misdeeds. The very truth that I wanted to pursue has turned out to be the most insignificant thing of all.

t_ch: 我当然知道这是必要的，是维系梦境稳定的手段，但……
t_en: I know all of this is necessary and a means to maintaining stability in the dreamscape. However...

t_ch: 为了匹诺康尼的福祉，我居然不得不和理想背道而驰…真是讽刺。
t_en: It's just... ironic that I have to betray my own ideals for the sake of Penacony.

t_ch: 唉…其实，我当年并没有想过要做一个裱糊匠。
t_en: *sigh* I've never actually thought about being a reality fixer-upper before.

t_ch: 我的梦想是当一名调查记者，寻找宇宙间被掩盖的真相。让大家不再因信息差而受到误导。
t_en: My dream was always to be an investigative reporter, chasing down hidden truths throughout the cosmos so that people would no longer be misled by low-quality information.

t_ch: 可由于「万界之癌」的影响，我只能留在梦境里，帮助家族文过饰非。我一直追求的真相反倒成了最无所谓的东西。
t_en: But thanks to the Cancer of All Worlds, all I can do is remain here in the dreamscape and help The Family smooth over their misdeeds. The very truth that I wanted to pursue has turned out to be the most insignificant thing of all.

t_ch: 我当然知道这是必要的，是维系梦境稳定的手段，但……
t_en: I know all of this is necessary and a means to maintaining stability in the dreamscape. However...

t_ch: 为了匹诺康尼的福祉，我居然不得不和理想背道而驰…真是讽刺。
t_en: It's just... ironic that I have to betray my own ideals for the sake of Penacony.

t_ch: 「黄金的时刻」又度过了安宁的一瞬…真好。
t_en: Another instant of peace passed in the Golden Hour... Wonderful.

t_ch: 啊，「黄金的时刻」又度过了安宁的一瞬…真好。
t_en: Ah, another instant of peace passed in the Golden Hour... Wonderful.

t_ch: 我感受到了，这就是家族的美梦。它也许不能满足所有人的期待，但至少能深深地打动一部分人。
t_en: I can feel that this is The Family's dream. It might not meet everyone's expectations, but at least it can leave a deep impression on some of the people.

t_ch: 看来我的工作确有价值——没什么比这更值得庆贺了！
t_en: Looks like my work has some value after all — and nothing's worth celebrating more than that!

t_ch: 「惊梦电视台」和怪物的下落…
t_en: Dreamjolt TV and the monster's whereabouts...

t_ch: 这个啊，幸得你的鼎力相助，匹诺康尼电视台已经恢复了往日的秩序。
t_en: Ah, that. Well, thanks to your kind efforts and assistance, the Penacony TV station has been restored to its former order.

t_ch: 乌玛压低了声音。
t_en: Uma lowers their voice.

t_ch: 家族派人去调查了，报告上说，那个出现在大屏幕上的「怪物」，实际上是观众们负面情绪的集合体。
t_en: The Family sent people to investigate, the report said that the "monster" that appeared on the big screen was actually just an aggregated concentration of angry emotions from viewers.

t_ch: 以前不时有观众反馈说，电视台的节目「太老土了」「毫无节目效果」，可惜台里也不重视……
t_en: We used to have viewers occasionally contact us saying that the stations shows were "outdated" or "not dramatic enough." Unfortunately, the station never took them seriously...

t_ch: 有了这次教训，电视台也不敢敷衍了事了，一连开了几档新节目，哈…监制他们有得忙了。
t_en: Well, that's a lesson learned. The station will be wary about going through the motions and just randomly pumping out new shows all the time. Hah... The producers better get busy!

t_ch: 咳咳…内部消息，不要跟其他人说。
t_en: Ahem... I mustn't divulge internal matters to outsiders.

t_ch: 嘘…别打岔。
t_en: Shh... don't interrupt!

t_ch: 仔细听……
t_en: Listen carefully...

t_ch: 听什么？
t_en: To what?

t_ch: …你感受不到它的美妙，是吧？
t_en: ...You can't feel the beauty of it, can you?

t_ch: （尝试聆听）
t_en: (Try and listen)

t_ch: …你听不出它的妙处，是吧？
t_en: ...You can't hear the splendor of it, can you?

t_ch: 可惜，你和这里的其他人一样。你们和真正美好的事物离得这么近，却主动选择视而不见，宁愿顺着自己廉价的欲望而活。
t_en: What a pity, you're just the same as everyone else here. You're oh-so-close to truly beautiful things, but you choose to turn a blind eye — and instead living based on your own cheap desires.

t_ch: 看看这地方，它哪里还有一点梦想之城的样子？放眼望去，周围尽是些习惯了靡衣偷食的家伙。他们早就忘了「梦想」为何物，来到这里只是为了享乐和纵欲。
t_en: Just take a look at this place. How exactly is it like a dreamscape anymore? There are people living hand-to-mouth as far as the eye can see. They've long forgotten what "dreaming" actually is, and come here just for pleasure and overindulgence.

t_ch: 没必要这么愤世嫉俗。
t_en: There's no need to be so jaded.

t_ch: 愤世嫉俗？不对，只是单纯的失望而已。
t_en: Jaded? No, this is merely disappointment.

t_ch: 那你有什么建议？
t_en: So what do you suggest?

t_ch: 我？我什么建议都给不了，家族的人也听不进去我的话。我能做的就只有发发牢骚而已。
t_en: Me? I can't give out any advice and The Family doesn't listen to me. All I can do is vent, that's it.

t_ch: 在我年轻的时候，我曾为了这座梦想之城而歌唱——那时的匹诺康尼和现在很不一样。
t_en: When I was younger, I sang about this city of dreams — back then, Penacony was vastly different from now.

t_ch: 看到远处那座宏伟的建筑了吗？那是匹诺康尼大歌剧院，十二个梦境时分的中轴，也是我曾经献声的舞台。
t_en: See that magnificent building off in the distance? That's the Penacony's grand theater, the central axis of the twelve dreamscape hours. It's also where I once used to take the stage and sing.

t_ch: 能在那个舞台上展现天赋是多么令人羡艳的一件事，你恐怕没有概念吧？聚光灯每每亮起，我能感受到来自银河间上亿双眼睛的热切注视。
t_en: I'm guessing you have no idea of how jealous people can get when you showcase your talents up there on that stage, right? Every time the spotlights flickered on, I could feel the fiery gaze of a million eyes from all across the cosmos.

t_ch: …但我从未被压力击垮过，一次都没有。我的每一次演出都是完美的。他们说我就是为歌唱而生的，说我就是声乐本身。
t_en: ...But I was never overwhelmed by pressure, not even once. Every performance was perfect. They said that I was born to sing, that I was the voice of music itself.

t_ch: 我创造的每个音节都能激起人们的共鸣，我时常能瞥见人群中眼含泪光的观众。我能在他们的目光里捕捉到纯粹的「梦想」——那个时代很不一样……
t_en: Every syllable I created resonated with people, and I'd often catch glimpses of teary-eyed audience members. I could capture the purity of the "dream" in their eyes — that was a really different era...

t_ch: 我想跟你聊聊…
t_en: I'd like to chat with you...

t_ch: 说吧，但我不觉得能和你有什么共同语言。
t_en: Speak, but I don't think we have anything in common.

t_ch: 你现在不再唱歌了吗？
t_en: Don't you sing anymore?

t_ch: …是谁说的？谁说我不再唱歌了？
t_en: ...Who said that? Who said I don't sing anymore?

t_ch: 咳、咳咳…咳！
t_en: Cough, cough... cough!

t_ch: 卡什女士激烈地咳嗽了起来，你的提问似乎刺激到了她。
t_en: Lady Cash coughs violently. Seems like your question has irritated her.

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 咳咳…咳……
t_en: Cough, cough... cough...

t_ch: …没什么，我不需要别人同情。
t_en: ...It's nothing. I don't need any sympathy.

t_ch: 去告诉他们，让那些质疑我的人都记住——我会再次发声的。我会回到舞台上，然后证明给他们看…让他们知道什么才是不朽的歌声。
t_en: Tell them, and don't let those who doubt me forget: I will sing again. I will return to the stage and prove myself to them... I'll show them what epic singing sounds like.

t_ch: 「梦想」在这个时代依旧存在。
t_en: "Dreams" still exist in this era.

t_ch: 也许吧…但我已经很久没看到过那种眼神了。
t_en: Maybe... But I haven't seen that look in anyone's eye for a long time.

t_ch: 歌颂梦想的年代已经结束了。现在那些所谓的歌手，任他们如何模仿和假装，也复制不出那个伟大时代的灵魂。
t_en: The age of singing about dreams is over. The so-called singers nowadays can't match the soul of that golden era, no matter how much they try and copy.

t_ch: 你怎么评价现在的音乐？
t_en: How do you rate modern music?

t_ch: 庸俗，糜烂。缺乏审美，词不达意。
t_en: Vulgar, decadent. It lacks melody and the lyrics fail to convey any meaning.

t_ch: 唱给公子哥听的无病呻吟，唱给贵妇人听的情情爱爱。
t_en: Singing about nonsensical sentiments for the young masters, and singing about lovey-dovey junk for the ladies.

t_ch: 音乐的黄金年代已经彻底结束了，连一点高贵的回响都没剩下。可悲……
t_en: The golden age of music is completely over. Not even a single notable echo lingers. How tragic...

t_ch: 就先说到这儿吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 回头见。
t_en: See you later.

t_ch: …不见也好，我不在意。
t_en: ...Or not, I don't care.

t_ch: 卡什女士瞥了你一眼，一言未发，继续凝望着远方的歌剧院。
t_en: Lady Cash shoots you a glance and continues to gaze in silence at the theater off in the distance.

t_ch: 你从她的余光中看出了一丝惆怅，还有一丝…恨怨。
t_en: You detect a hint of melancholy out of the corner of her eyes, and a trace of... resentment.

t_ch: 即便是一模一样的音乐曲调，假如其诞生的年代不同，它所代表的意义和价值也会完全颠覆。
t_en: Even if the tune is exactly the same, if it was born in a different era, then the meaning and values it represents will be completely subverted.

t_ch: 当今的歌手多数都拥有非常成熟的技巧，这点我承认。但前提是他们踩在了巨人的肩膀上，可以学习的样本比过去要多得多。
t_en: Singers today mostly rely on very mature techniques, I can admit that. But they are standing on the shoulders of giants, and there are so many mentors to learn from nowadays.

t_ch: 但技巧永远只能是技巧，技巧没法给的作品创造好的立意，更没法赋予作品时代的厚重感。
t_en: But technique will only ever be technical — they cannot create concepts for work, nor embody any work with a sense of zeitgeist.

t_ch: 所以我不能认可他们的歌声，我决不认可……
t_en: So I cannot endorse their singing. I just can't...

t_ch: 卡什女士面露愁容，她因岁月而稍显浑浊的双眼映射出城市的灯彩，似乎再下一秒就要有泪水留下。
t_en: Lady Cash wears a troubled expression, and her eyes, slightly dimmed by the passage of time, reflect the city's lights, as if tears are about to fall in the next moment.

t_ch: 你能清晰地感受到，即便是在梦中，某些难以放下的执念仍在纠缠着她。
t_en: You can clearly feel despite being in a dream that there's an unshakable obsession that still haunts her.

t_ch: 知更鸟……
t_en: Robin...

t_ch: 谁？
t_en: Who?

t_ch: 知更鸟……
t_en: Robin...

t_ch: 你认识她？
t_en: Do you know her?

t_ch: 认识？何止认识……
t_en: Know her? I more than just know her...

t_ch: 我的学生，我亲手培养出来的好学生，她拿走了属于我的一切——舞台，光芒，声音，前程……
t_en: She was my student, the prize student I taught with my own two hands. She took away everything that belonged to me: The stage, the lights, my voice, my future...

t_ch: 但我能责怪谁呢？这一切都是我咎由自取…我明白，我其实比谁都明白……
t_en: But who can I blame but myself for everything... I understand I brought it all upon me, and I know that better than anyone else...

t_ch: 颂歌神啊，为什么要愚弄我？既然赐予我好为人师的慷慨，又何必再给我安上一颗善妒的心——
t_en: O god of serenades, why do you mock me so? You've blessed me with the gift of being a passionate teacher, why then, do you burden me with a jealous heart...

t_ch: ——然后又要让这嗓疾夺走我的音律，让我不得不放弃我人生中唯一热爱的东西…颂歌神啊，你何等残酷……
t_en: ...and then let this illness take away my melody, forcing me to give up the one thing I've ever loved in my life... O god of serenades, how cruel you are...

t_ch: 拿去吧…把这东西拿去吧。它对我来说已经没有意义了，留它在身边…除了折磨自己之外…毫无作用。
t_en: Take it... Keep it for yourself. It no longer has any meaning for me, and there's no point in keeping it around anymore... other than to torture me...

t_ch: 知更鸟，我亲手培养出来的好学生……
t_en: Robin, my student who I raised with my own hands...

t_ch: 她拿走了属于我的一切——舞台，光芒，声音，前程……
t_en: She took away everything that belonged to me: The stage, the lights, my voice, my future...

t_ch: 但我还能责怪谁呢？这都是我咎由自取……
t_en: But who can I blame but myself for everything... I brought this upon myself...

t_ch: 卡什女士面露愠色。你不知道她的怨气指向何方，但至少现在她还表现得十分克制，没有丧失身为旧日明星的那份优雅。
t_en: Lady Cash looks sullen. You don't know where she is directing her resentment, but for now, she is demonstrating incredible restraint in not losing the elegance of her former celebrity.

t_ch: 我不认识你——不过既然你有那个财力来到匹诺康尼，就起码该懂点基本的礼节。
t_en: I don't know about you — but seeing as you have the financial means to visit Penacony, you ought to understand the basics of etiquette.

t_ch: 不管你出身哪个星系，这样盯着人看都称不上礼貌。
t_en: No matter which galaxy you come from, staring at people like that is not going to be considered polite.
Tsk...

t_ch: 真是虚伪……
t_en: What a hypocrite...

t_ch: 你有什么心事吗？
t_en: What's on your mind?

t_ch: 我甚至不认识你，为什么要和你分享我的感受？
t_en: I don't even know you. Why should I share my feelings with you?

t_ch: 为什么骂我？
t_en: Why are you scolding me?

t_ch: 心虚的人才喜欢对号入座，而我甚至不知道你是谁。
t_en: Only those with a guilty conscience would take these comments as personal attacks. And I don't even know who you are.

t_ch: 念什么师生之情…以为我会相信这种话吗？那家伙，写下这几行字的时候脸上一定还挂着得意的笑吧。
t_en: Talking about teachers and students... as though I'd believe all that? This person must've had the biggest, self-congratulatory grin on their face while writing that.

t_ch: 不过是废纸一张，我不稀罕。以后我再也不想听到和她有关的消息了——永远都不！
t_en: But it's just a scrap of paper, nothing precious. In the future, I don't want to hear anything at all about her — never again!

t_ch: 唉…其实，我当年并没有想过要做一个裱糊匠。
t_en: *sigh* I've never actually thought about being a reality fixer-upper before.

t_ch: 我的梦想是当一名调查记者，寻找宇宙间被掩盖的真相。让大家不再因信息差而受到误导。
t_en: My dream was always to be an investigative reporter, chasing down hidden truths throughout the cosmos so that people would no longer be misled by low-quality information.

t_ch: 可由于「万界之癌」的影响，我只能留在梦境里，帮助家族文过饰非。我一直追求的真相反倒成了最无所谓的东西。
t_en: But thanks to the Cancer of All Worlds, all I can do is remain here in the dreamscape and help The Family smooth over their misdeeds. The very truth that I wanted to pursue has turned out to be the most insignificant thing of all.

t_ch: 我当然知道这是必要的，是维系梦境稳定的手段，但……
t_en: I know all of this is necessary and a means to maintaining stability in the dreamscape. However...

t_ch: 为了匹诺康尼的福祉，我居然不得不和理想背道而驰…真是讽刺。
t_en: It's just... ironic that I have to betray my own ideals for the sake of Penacony.

t_ch: 什么师生之情，培育之恩……
t_en: So what — a teacher and a student, this nurturing kindness...

t_ch: 都是蛊惑人心的鬼话！她最擅长这个了…她从小就是那种孩子。
t_en: It's all a bucket of brainwashing balderdash! This is what she's best at... She's been like this since young.

t_ch: 卡什女士哼唱起一段轻巧的旋律，和远处歌剧院飘来的乐声相得益彰。
t_en: Lady Cash hums a jaunty tune that harmonizes with the melody coming out of the theater in the distance.

t_ch: 有一瞬间，你从她身上看到了那个曾经骄傲的影子，纵情地施展着歌喉、放着光——尽管你未曾亲眼目睹过她当年的风采。
t_en: For a moment, you can see her once-proud countenance putting her voice and dance moves to full use — even though you never even witnessed her grace back in the day.

t_ch: 「斑斓的夜，依偎在梦的陲边；驻足房顶，等候着奇迹浮现…」
t_en: "A gorgeous night sky, nestled on a dream and a prayer; standing atop the roof, waiting for miracles to appear..."

t_ch: 「飞鸟已逝，羽折在寥旷之天；永驻时光，待来生与你再见…」
t_en: "The swooping bird has passed away, feathers folded in the sky; a product of its time, awaiting a meeting in the next life..."

t_ch: 卡什女士自顾自地哼唱了起来。因为某些原因，她的声音不再婉转悦耳，但却带着一种难以言说的、引人入胜的魔力，让人忍不住驻足想要聆听。
t_en: Lady Cash starts humming along by herself. For some reason, her voice is no longer melodious but still has this indescribable and captivating magic to it that makes people want to stop and listen.

t_ch: 这美妙的一刻只属于她自己，任谁都不该贸然打断。
t_en: This magical moment belongs to her and her alone, and no one can interrupt it.

t_ch: 会动的道具！这可比动物园有趣多了，快来看看吧！
t_en: A movable prop! This is more interesting than a zoo. Come on and have a look!

t_ch: 哟，这位客人，你也对道具表演感兴趣？那你来对地方了。
t_en: Hey, interested in a prop performance? Then you're at the right place.

t_ch: 我是维塔利，匹诺康尼最神奇的道具训练师！瞧瞧，我身后这些就是最新的训练成果。
t_en: I am Vitali, the most amazing prop trainer in Penacony! Look behind me! These are my latest training results.

t_ch: 训练…道具？
t_en: Training... props?

t_ch: 道具表演？
t_en: Prop performance?

t_ch: 是啊，您应该见过「美梦剧团」，对吧？那些可爱的小东西着实为梦境增色不少。
t_en: Yes, I believe you've seen before Sweet Dreams Troupe? Those adorable items have added colors to the Dreamscapes.

t_ch: 但并非所有道具都能成为「美梦剧团」——为了确保每名客人都能得到最好的梦境体验，只有最惹人喜爱的物件才会被家族改造。
t_en: However, not all props can become part of the Sweet Dreams Troupe. To ensure only the best Dreamscape can be experienced by our customers, only loveable items will be remodeled by The Family.

t_ch: 至于一件道具为什么会落选，理由可能五花八门：有些缺乏灵性，有些品控低劣；有些尚不成熟，还有些年纪太大——哦，这么说可能会引起误会——应该叫「使用年限过长」。
t_en: As for why an item is not selected, the reasons can be many: Some lack spirituality, some have poor quality control, some are immature, and some are too old — oh, this word might be misleading — it should be "past its useful life."

t_ch: 而驯物师的工作，就是训练这些落选的物品，帮助它们实现「动起来」的梦想。
t_en: As a props master, I train the rejected items and help them to fulfill their dreams of "moving."

t_ch: 嘴动不如行动，正好这个美梦脆筒还没训练完，我直接给你现场演示一遍。可别眨眼~
t_en: Let me put my words into action. Here's an untrained Dreamy Cone. I'll give you a live demonstration. Don't blink~

t_ch: 维塔利不知从哪儿掏出一条鞭子，狠狠抽打在脆筒身上。呼呼的破风声让你不由得眯起了眼。
t_en: Vitali throws out a whip out of nowhere and hit the cone hard with it. The whipcracking sounds make you squint your eyes.

t_ch: 变！给我变！
t_en: Transform! I say, transform!

t_ch: 这样的抽打持续了好几分钟才停止——不是因为善心大发，而是因为他用尽了气力。
t_en: The whipping only stops after several minutes. No, it isn't because the trainer decides to let the cone go, but because he has exhausted all his energy.

t_ch: 呼，真累人呐…瞧，这就是我每天的工作。
t_en: Whew, that's tiring... Look, that's my job every day.

t_ch: 这样真有用吗？
t_en: Is this really useful?

t_ch: …你没事吧？
t_en: ...Are you okay?

t_ch: …啊，谢谢，我还好，就是腰有点疼。
t_en: ...Ah, thank you. I'm fine. Only my back hurts a little.

t_ch: 吃得苦中苦，方为物上物。想要让道具拥有运动能力，必须连续数月、持之以恒地鞭策才行。
t_en: No pain, no gain. To get these items moving, one must persevere and whip them for countless months.

t_ch: 为了它们的梦想，我可算得上鞠躬尽瘁——要是它们的表演不能让我满意，那真就太不识好歹了。
t_en: I've exhausted every fiber of my body to make their dreams come true — if they were to disappoint me in their performance, they would be ungrateful.

t_ch: 感觉像是虐待…
t_en: It feels like you're torturing them...

t_ch: 挺好的，就是有点怪。
t_en: Okay... It still sounds a little weird.

t_ch: 咳…好吧，我承认训练活动的观感可能有些不尽人意。
t_en: Ahem... fine. I admit that the training routine may not be acceptable to some.

t_ch: 不过不用担心，这套方法是完全符合家族规定的——反正不会动的道具连智械都算不上，根本没有物权。
t_en: But fret not. This method is fully compliant with The Family regulations. Anyway, immovable items aren't Intellitron, so they basically don't have any rights.

t_ch: 意思是会动之后就有了？
t_en: You mean to imply they will have that after they move?

t_ch: …咳，不是，我就随便编了个词，你别当真啊。
t_en: ...Ahem, no. I just came up with that term myself. Ignore me.

t_ch: 说到底，不管是「美梦剧团」还是这些家伙——不过都是供人玩乐的物件罢了！
t_en: In any case, regardless of the Sweet Dreams Troupe or these items, they are just things for human entertainment!

t_ch: 我认识那么几个驯物师同僚，工作得都有些魔怔了，居然会把物品当作自己的伙伴…简直失了分寸！
t_en: I know of a few props masters who went nuts from their work and treated these items as their partners... They have gone overboard!

t_ch: 总之，你是对物件心生怜悯也好，单纯好奇心作祟也罢，都可以随时来我这里观看节目——保准让你笑掉袜子！
t_en: All in all, whether you took pity on these items or were just simply curious about them, feel free to come here for the performances — guaranteed to make you laugh your socks off!

t_ch: 你都有什么演出？
t_en: What kind of performances do you have?

t_ch: 目前能出场的三位台柱分别是路灯小姐、玻璃瓶先生和垃圾桶怪客。客人，您想看哪一个？
t_en: For now, we have only three stalwarts — Miss Street Lamp, Mr. Glass Bottle, and the Trashcan Monster. Which one would you like to watch, customer?

t_ch: 路灯。
t_en: Street Lamp.

t_ch: 客人好眼光！路灯小姐可是深受各路富商的喜欢。我这就把它给您带来。
t_en: Great choice! Miss Street Lamp is the most favorable among the rich merchants. I'll call her in now.

t_ch: 维塔利将路灯牵了过来。一见面，它就礼貌地向你点头问好。
t_en: Vitali leads the Street Lamp to you. When your eyes meet, it nods and greets you politely.

t_ch: 这是一只又瘦又高的路灯。打招呼时，它雪白的脖颈总是随着点头前后摆动，仿佛在对你招手。
t_en: It is a slender and tall street lamp. When it greets you, its snow-white neck sways front and back, as if beckoning to you.

t_ch: 刚才那是路灯小姐唯一掌握的节目，已经为您表演完了。
t_en: And that's the only action Miss Street Lamp has. She's done with her performance.

t_ch: …就这？
t_en: ...That's it?

t_ch: 不要怀疑路灯小姐的名气，没看见这个招呼打得…非常吉利吗？
t_en: Do not doubt Miss Street Lamp's fame. Didn't you see the way she greets you? Don't you think that's... a good sign?

t_ch: 那它为什么讨人喜欢啊？
t_en: Then why is she everyone's favorite?

t_ch: 咳，这是因为路灯小姐打招呼的方式…非常吉利。
t_en: Ahem, that's because the way Miss Street Lamp greets others looks like... a very good sign.

t_ch: 经商的客人们总觉得这个动作能招财，所以每次打赏都出手阔绰。
t_en: The merchants believe her action will bring them plenty of fortune, so they are always very generous when tipping her.

t_ch: 既然能赚到这么多钱…我是说，既然有这么多客人喜欢，何必费劲去想别的节目呢？
t_en: Since she brings me so much wealth... I mean, since she's everyone's favorite, why is there a need for me to think of other actions?

t_ch: 你再次看向路灯小姐。它的脖颈是那样修长，挂在上面或许会很舒服……
t_en: You look at Miss Street Lamp again. It must be comfortable to hang on its long and slender neck...

t_ch: 玻璃瓶。
t_en: Glass Bottle.

t_ch: 客人好眼光！玻璃瓶先生虽然还没路演过，但它已经在训练中养成了出色的弹跳力——我这就把它给您带来。
t_en: Great choice! Even though Mr. Glass Bottle hasn't been on a show before, it has mastered a spectacular bouncing ability. Let me invite it out now.

t_ch: 维塔利将玻璃瓶牵了过来。一见面，它直接空翻了三个跟头……
t_en: Vitali leads Mr. Glass Bottle to you. The moment your eyes meet, it flips three times in the air...

t_ch: 然后华丽地落在地上，摔裂了。
t_en: And grandiosely lands itself on the floor before it breaks into many pieces.

t_ch: 哎哟，怎么搞的？它训练时可不这样啊…看来还是练得少了。
t_en: Oh, what's wrong with it? It performed so well during training... seems like I've not trained them hard enough.

t_ch: 客人，您见笑了！下次我肯定会训练出一只专业素质更硬的玻璃瓶。
t_en: Customer, pardon him! I will certainly bring you a well-trained glass bottle next time!

t_ch: 这瓶子质量不行。
t_en: The quality of this glass bottle needs to be improved.

t_ch: 您说的对！下次我一定换一家质量更好的。
t_en: You're right! I'll get a better quality bottle next time.

t_ch: 它还有救吗？
t_en: Can it still be salvaged?

t_ch: 不过是只酒瓶子而已，有修的工夫还不如去买个新的，您说呢？
t_en: It's just a liquor bottle. Instead of spending the effort to mend it, why not just buy a new one, right?

t_ch: 垃圾桶。
t_en: A trashcan.

t_ch: 客人好眼光！垃圾桶怪客是我们这里人气最高、表演效果也最好的道具！我这就把它给您带来。
t_en: Good choice! The Trashcan Monster is our most popular item with the best performance! Let me bring it out now.

t_ch: 在一轮强劲的音乐中，维塔利将垃圾桶怪客牵了过来——一只有手有脚的垃圾桶。
t_en: With loud music playing in the background, Vitali leads trashcan Monster out — it's a trashcan with hands and legs.

t_ch: 它果然一幅明星做派，压根没打算向你问好。
t_en: Indeed, it has an air about it and has no intention to greet you.

t_ch: 看这结实的肌肉、霸气的姿态和深邃的眼神，就知道它是垃圾桶中的极品了！
t_en: Looking at its firm muscles, dominant posture, and deep eyes, you know it must be an extraordinary trashcan!

t_ch: 垃圾桶怪客，为我们热舞一曲吧！
t_en: Trashcan Monster, come on and give us your best move!

t_ch: 垃圾桶不发一言，只是听话地跳起了不知名的舞蹈，桶盖转了一圈又一圈。
t_en: Without a word, the trashcan obediently spins round and round, doing an unknown dance.

t_ch: 冥冥之中，你感受到了一种…熟悉，仿佛它是你失散多年的{M#兄弟}{F#姐妹}一样。
t_en: Somehow, you feel a sense of... familiarity. It's as if the trashcan before you is your long-lost {M#brother}{F#sister}.

t_ch: 怎么样？这位客人，演出还满意吗？
t_en: How was it? Are you satisfied with the performance, Customer?

t_ch: 我都想上去共舞一曲了。
t_en: It makes me wanna dance with it too.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，没想到它的表演这么合你胃口！
t_en: Hahaha, I never thought that you would like such a performance!

t_ch: 完全没问题！等我下去以后再训练训练，未来就能推出「双人共舞」节目了！
t_en: No problem! I will give it more training so that it can do a dance duet next time!

t_ch: 有点怪异。
t_en: It's weird.

t_ch: 呃，毕竟这段舞蹈的「道具特色」比较重，一般人的确不好配合。
t_en: Er, this dance may be too focused on the "item's characteristics" and it's hard for ordinary people to follow.

t_ch: 您的反馈我已经收到了，等我下去以后再训练训练，未来就能推出「双人共舞」节目了！
t_en: I accept your feedback and will train it further. Next time, it will be able to do a two-person dance performance!

t_ch: 美梦脆筒。
t_en: Dreamy Cone.

t_ch: 呃…可以是可以，不过这件道具的训练还没结束，效果可能……
t_en: Er... Of course. But this item has yet to be trained, and the effect may be...

t_ch: 算了，有什么大不了的？我可不能让客人失望！您稍等……
t_en: Never mind. What's the big deal? I mustn't disappoint my customer! Please wait a minute...

t_ch: 维塔利将美梦脆筒牵到了你面前。
t_en: Vitali leads Dreamy Cone to you.

t_ch: 这就是我最用心的训练对象，「美梦剧团」未来的新星，匹诺康尼人见人爱的脆筒舞者——现在，动起来吧！
t_en: I've trained this item with all my effort. It is the future star of Sweet Dreams Troupe and Penacony's beloved Cone Dancer. Now, show us your moves!

t_ch: 脆筒无动于衷。
t_en: The Cone doesn't move an inch.

t_ch: 给我动！动啊！
t_en: I say, move! Move it!

t_ch: 维塔利扬起鞭子抽打在脆筒身上，后者却全然不为所动。
t_en: Vitali raises his whip and hits the cone with it. However, there is no reaction from the cone.

t_ch: 「脆筒舞者」仿佛化作了奶油身躯的战士，蛋筒底座的巨碑。它的不屈精神是草莓味的——*吸溜*。
t_en: The Cone Dancer seems to have transformed into a cream-bodied warrior, a giant monument at the base of the egg cone. Its indomitable fighting spirit is strawberry-flavored — *slurp*

t_ch: （一口吃掉脆筒）
t_en: (Eat the cone in one bite)

t_ch: 你一口将脆筒吞下——这就是它不听话的下场！
t_en: You swallowed the cone... This is the punishment for not listening!

t_ch: 迎着维塔利诧异的目光，你肆意感受着脆筒在你唇齿间留下的甜蜜。
t_en: As Vitali stares at you in bewilderment, you willfully taste the sweetness of the crunchy cone on your lips.

t_ch: 整个梦境都清静了，只剩下一声又惊又气的嚎呼……
t_en: The entire Dreamscape becomes quiet. You can only hear the surprised and angry wails...
<size=28>NOOOOOO!!!</size>

t_ch: 你把我训练的脆筒给、给吃了！我饶不了你啊啊啊！
t_en: You a—ate my Cone in Training! I will not forgive you!

t_ch: （静观其变）
t_en: (Stay calm and observe)

t_ch: 这样的拉锯战持续了有一会儿，维塔利终于败下阵来。他擦了擦汗，叹出一口气。
t_en: This tug-of-war goes on for a while until Vitali finally gives up. He wipes his perspiration and sighs.

t_ch: 唉，看来脆筒舞者今天是不打算听话了…果然欠训练的物品就是上不来台面。
t_en: *sigh* It seems like the Cone Dancer isn't planning to obey me today... Indeed, under-trained items should never go on stage.

t_ch: 抱歉了客人，等我把它调教完成后再为您表演吧。
t_en: I'm sorry, Customer. Allow me to complete its training before it performs for you again.

t_ch: 先这样吧
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 没关系，您想什么时候来都可以。
t_en: Don't worry. You can come here anytime.

t_ch: 你以为这就完了吗？你以为你可以吃了我的脆筒后扬长而去吗？不！你不可以！
t_en: Do you think that's it? Do you think you can leave after you ate my cone? No! Impossible!

t_ch: 你摧毁了我灌注心血的道具，我会永远铭记这一刻！你永远都别想让我原谅你，永远！
t_en: You've destroyed my precious item, and I will remember it forever! I'll never forgive you. Never!

t_ch: 要是面对一般人，他或许真能永远保持一颗愤怒的心，但对你不行——是时候让他见识下钟表把戏的厉害了。
t_en: If you're just an ordinary patron, maybe he can be mad at you forever. But not if it's you — time to show him the power of the Clockwork.

t_ch: 脆筒…我的脆筒…呜呜呜——
t_en: Cone... My precious cone... Waaaaaah —

t_ch: 男人的悲伤化作眼泪夺眶而出，壮硕的身躯随着哭泣颤抖…看来你需要其他情绪才能让这件事翻篇。
t_en: The man's sorrow transforms into tears rolling down his face. His muscular body trembles as he weeps... It seems like you need to use other emotions to settle this problem.

t_ch: 我的愤怒，突然消失了…即便如此，你也必须赔偿我的损失。
t_en: My anger has vanished all of a sudden... Even so, you should still compensate me for my loss.

t_ch: …这个脆筒我训了好久，怎么也值个一亿信用点吧？
t_en: ...I've trained this cone for a long time. Shouldn't you compensate me a <unbreak>100</unbreak> million credits?

t_ch: 不赔钱，这事儿就不算完！
t_en: If you don't pay up, I'm not going to let you off!

t_ch: 男人的愤怒转变为理智，而理智产生了贪欲…看来你需要其他情绪才能让这件事翻篇。
t_en: The man turns his anger into rational thinking, and his rationality produces greed... It seems like you will need to use other emotions to solve this problem.

t_ch: 话说回来，那个脆筒的味道怎么样？
t_en: By the way, how does the cone taste?

t_ch: 很美味。
t_en: Delectable.

t_ch: 很好，它这一辈子也算值得。
t_en: Great. Its life amounted to something in the end.

t_ch: 一般吧。
t_en: Average.

t_ch: 或许你更适合其他口味。
t_en: Perhaps you prefer other flavors.

t_ch: 真难吃！
t_en: Horrible!

t_ch: 随便你怎么说，反正也没有第二个了。
t_en: Whatever. I don't have another cone for you anyway.

t_ch: 看你刚才吃得那么来劲，我也萌生了来一个脆筒的想法。就在这时，我突然意识到一件事……
t_en: I noticed you were enjoying it just now, and an idea about cone struck my mind. At that moment, I suddenly realized one thing...

t_ch: ——是啊，既然脆筒本就是用来吃的，那我为什么要对你吃脆筒的行为感到愤怒呢？物品就该满足人的需求才对。
t_en: ...You're right. Since a cone is meant to be eaten, why should I be angry that you ate it? The purpose of an item is to satisfy a human's needs, no?

t_ch: 这座城市里还有许多脆筒，我再去找一个训就是了，反正之前那家伙也不怎么听话。
t_en: There are still many cones in this city, and I can just train another one. The previous one wasn't obedient anyway.

t_ch: 哦对，你看起来很喜欢吃脆筒的样子——拿去吧，别再吃我训好的东西了。
t_en: Right, you seem to enjoy eating cones. Take these and stop eating my trained props.

t_ch: 这些道具多么可爱啊，比动物有趣多了，对吧？
t_en: Look at how adorable these props are. They are much more interesting than animals. Am I right?

t_ch: 哎呀，刚才那些话都别往心里去——我其实是很爱惜这些道具，也很热爱匹诺康尼的。
t_en: Oh my, please don't mind my words — I love these props like my own, and I'm a fan of Penacony.

t_ch: 一抹笑容重新出现在维塔利脸上，不知有几分真假。
t_en: A dashing smile appears on Vitali's face once more, though you can't tell if it's honest.

t_ch: 这些道具，一个个都太没用了！
t_en: These props are useless!

t_ch: 只要这些道具还能动，它们就该拿出全部的力气取悦观众，为我赚钱！
t_en: As long as they can still move, they should do their best to entertain the audience and make money for me!

t_ch: 没用的东西，简直是废物！训练这么久都学不会！
t_en: Useless items! Garbage! They can't even get anything right after all that training!

t_ch: 那只破手套，已经过去六个月了，连个瓶盖都捡不起来！
t_en: That tattered glove can't even pick up a bottle cap after six months of training!

t_ch: 明明它只需要把钱从观众口袋里掏出来就行了。多简单的事啊，从来没做好过！
t_en: All it needs to do is pick the money from the audiences' pockets. It only has one job, and it can't do it!

t_ch: 你训练手套…去偷钱？
t_en: Did you train the glove... to steal?

t_ch: 真难听！什么叫偷，这是比喻，是催促观众快快打赏的比喻！
t_en: That's awful! What "steal"? That's just an indirect way to urge the audiences to give us tips!

t_ch: 再说了，物质的价值就在于满足人的需求。我的需求就是钱啊！钱！
t_en: Moreover, the value of these items lies in the fulfillment of human needs. My need is cash! Cash is king!

t_ch: 只要这些道具还能动，它们就该拿出全部的力气取悦观众，为我赚钱！除非它们能凭空变出信用点来——那倒是能省去中间那么多费力不讨好的步骤。
t_en: As long as they can still move, they should do their best to entertain the audience and make money for me! Unless they can magically conjure up some credits — that would save so many tedious steps in between.

t_ch: 我为训练这些道具花了那么多心血和时间，追求点回报难道有错吗？嗯？
t_en: To think I have spent so much effort and time to train these props, is it wrong for me to ask for a little reward? Huh?

t_ch: 到最后，只有不会说话的道具能听我倾诉……
t_en: In the end, only these voiceless props will listen to my grumbles...

t_ch: 它们就像我的…伙伴？唉，越说越可悲……
t_en: They are like my... partners? Boy, now I sound so pathetic...

t_ch: 回想起来，我在匹诺康尼…已经待了很久啊。
t_en: Come to think of it, I have been in Penacony... for quite a long time.

t_ch: 最开始，我是为了追逐梦想才来到这里的。结果现在呢？我连一开始的梦想是什么都记不起来了。
t_en: In the beginning, I was here to pursue my dream. But now? I don't even remember what was my dream.

t_ch: 名誉、金钱、快乐…那些吸引我视线的东西，似乎哪样都没能握在手中。
t_en: Fame, wealth, happiness... all these things attract me, yet I can't seem to get hold of any of them.

t_ch: 我是个失败者…就连真正愿意理解我的人都找不到……
t_en: I'm a failure... I can't even find someone who is willing to hear me out...

t_ch: 到最后，只有不会说话的道具能倾听我的诉说……
t_en: In the end, only voiceless props will listen to my grumbles...

t_ch: 它们就像我的…伙伴？唉，越说越可悲…得吃点东西缓缓心情才行。
t_en: They are like my... partners? Boy, now I sound so pathetic... I need some snacks to lift my mood.

t_ch: 回想起来，我在匹诺康尼…已经待了很久啊。
t_en: Come to think of it, I have been in Penacony... for quite a long time.

t_ch: 最开始，我是为了追逐梦想才来到这里的。结果现在呢？我连一开始的梦想是什么都记不起来了。
t_en: In the beginning, I was here to pursue my dream. But now? I don't even remember what was my dream.

t_ch: 名誉、金钱、快乐…那些吸引我视线的东西，似乎哪样都没能握在手中。
t_en: Fame, wealth, happiness... all these things attract me, yet I can't seem to get hold of any of them.

t_ch: 我是个失败者…就连真正愿意理解我的人都找不到……
t_en: I'm a failure... I can't even find someone who is willing to hear me out...

t_ch: 到最后，只有不会说话的道具能倾听我的诉说……
t_en: In the end, only voiceless props will listen to my grumbles...

t_ch: 它们就像我的…伙伴？唉，越说越可悲……
t_en: They are like my... partners? Boy, now I sound so pathetic...

t_ch: 嘿，这主意肯定能赚到大钱……
t_en: Hey, I'm sure I can make big bucks with this idea...

t_ch: 仔细想想，训练道具跟训练牲畜似乎也没什么不同，最后还是得能赚钱才行……
t_en: Come to think of it, there's no difference between training props and training animals. Most importantly, I must be able to earn money from it...

t_ch: 既然这样，干脆弄点更抓眼球的活动好了——让玻璃瓶走钢丝，让垃圾桶钻火圈什么的；把客人伺候舒服了，还愁钱不来吗？
t_en: Now that we've come to this, why not introduce more exciting performances — get the glass bottle to walk on tightrope and the trashcan to jump through fire hoops. Once the customers are happy, I won't have to worry about the money!

t_ch: 要是客人有猎奇心理的话，还可以让他们体验亲手训练道具的感觉……
t_en: And if the customers are keen to try new things, I can always let them experience the feeling of training props...

t_ch: 嘿，钱也赚了，活也干了，这主意还真不错。果然我一冷静下来，金点子就接二连三地往外涌啊~
t_en: Hey, that way, I can get both my money and my work done. What a great idea. I just need to stay calm and great money-making ideas just keep flowing~

t_ch: 不过仔细一想，我训出来的脆筒味道不错…或许改行卖脆筒也是个好主意？
t_en: But on second thought, the cone I trained tasted delicious... Maybe it would be a good idea to sell crunchy cones instead?

t_ch: 对职业的热忱消散后，维塔利的脑子里就只剩下了生意经。
t_en: Now that the passion for his career has melted away, Vitali's mind is only filled with business ideas.

t_ch: 仔细想想，训练道具跟训练牲畜似乎也没什么不同，最后还是得能赚钱才行……
t_en: Come to think of it, there's no difference between training props and training animals. Most importantly, I must be able to earn money from it...

t_ch: 既然这样，干脆弄点更抓眼球的活动好了——让玻璃瓶走钢丝，让垃圾桶钻火圈什么的；把客人伺候舒服了，还愁钱不来吗？
t_en: Now that we've come to this, why not introduce more exciting performances — get the glass bottle to walk on tightrope and the trashcan to jump through fire hoops. Once the customers are happy, I won't have to worry about the money!

t_ch: 要是客人有猎奇心理的话，还可以让他们体验亲手训练道具的感觉……
t_en: And if the customers are keen to try new things, I can always let them experience the feeling of training props...

t_ch: 嘿，钱也赚了，活也干了，这主意还真不错。果然我一冷静下来，金点子就接二连三地往外涌啊~
t_en: Hey, that way, I can get both my money and my work done. What a great idea. I just need to stay calm and great money-making ideas just keep flowing~

t_ch: 对职业的热忱消散后，维塔利的脑子里就只剩下了生意经。
t_en: Now that the passion for his career has melted away, Vitali's mind is only filled with business ideas.

t_ch: 你好啊客人，要观看维塔利的「动物」演出吗？
t_en: Hello, Customer. Do you want to watch Vitali's "Animals" Show?

t_ch: 我得走了。
t_en: I got to go.

t_ch: 呃…你是要找我吗？抱歉，我还以为你在看我身后的什么东西。
t_en: Er... Are you looking for me? Sorry, I thought you were looking at the things behind me.

t_ch: 你在这里做什么？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: …唉，你能问出这个问题，说明我还是太失败了。
t_en: ...Well, the fact that you can ask me this question shows that I'm still a total failure.

t_ch: …我是这里的看店伙计，负责盯着那些搞小动作的客人。除此以外，我也帮不上什么忙。
t_en: ...I'm here to look after the store and look out for customers with sticky fingers. Besides that, there's nothing I can do.

t_ch: 你是猎犬家系的吗？
t_en: Are you a member of the Bloodhound Family?

t_ch: 哈？我怎么可能是猎犬呢！那些家族护卫是多么可靠，多么威武…我大概这辈子都成不了那样的人吧。
t_en: Huh? How can I be a Bloodhound!? The Family's guards are so dependable and mighty... I doubt I can ever be like them.

t_ch: 这家店是做什么的？
t_en: What does this store sell?

t_ch: 只是个普通的商店而已，我家开的，主意来自我父母。
t_en: Just an ordinary store. It's a family business. Opening this store was my parents' idea.

t_ch: 他们行动力高得吓人：开店的想法是某天晚上突然冒出来的。我原以为只是说着玩，谁知道他们第二天中午就把事情都办妥了。
t_en: They're fast and furious; the idea of opening a store popped into their head one night. I thought they were kidding, who knew they'd have it all done by noon the next day.

t_ch: 现在他们每天都能享受天伦之乐，顺便赚点外快；我爸负责把货物带进梦境里，我妈负责用伶牙俐齿招呼客人——其乐融融不是吗？
t_en: Now they get to enjoy life every day and make a little extra cash on the side; my dad is responsible for bringing the goods into the Dreamscape and my eloquent mom is responsible for serving the customers — isn't it a perfect arrangement?

t_ch: 只有我无足轻重，就像路边的一块石头，哈哈……
t_en: Only I'm the redundant one, like a pebble on the street. Haha...

t_ch: 有些话想和你聊聊。
t_en: I need to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 好吧，反正我也没有其他事情可做……
t_en: Okay. I have nothing else to do at the moment anyway...

t_ch: 你父亲是什么样的人？
t_en: What kind of person is your dad?

t_ch: 我父亲是一位智械。他曾是巡海游侠的一员，当年提着两把采矿钻歼灭过一整船的海盗，人送外号「铁皮煞客」。
t_en: Dad is an Intellitron. He used to be a Galaxy Ranger, who wiped out an entire shipload of pirates with two mining drills. His nickname was "Tin Fury."

t_ch: 即便如今已经退休，他钛合金脑壳的光芒还是那么耀眼，很难不让人敬畏。
t_en: Even though he retired, the glow of his titanium skull is still dazzling. It's hard not to be in awe of him.

t_ch: 我想…我大概这辈子都成不了像他那样的人吧。
t_en: I think... I can never be a person like him.

t_ch: 你母亲是什么样的人？
t_en: What kind of person is your mom?

t_ch: 我的母亲…她是一位体贴、善解人意的家庭妇女。不过从前的她比现在要潇洒许多。
t_en: Mom... she's a considerate and kind housewife, but she used to be a dashing young lady.

t_ch: 她曾经是个宇宙行商，靠着超凡毅力打出了自己的一片市场。直到有次遇上了海盗……
t_en: She was a universal traveling merchant, carving out a niche for herself with her extraordinary perseverance. It wasn't until she encountered the pirates...

t_ch: 那也是她和我父亲的初次相识，一个标准的英雄救美故事。后来嘛……
t_en: That was the first time my dad and mom met — a typical damsel in distress story. Thereafter...

t_ch: 据说只有爱才能锁住一个自由的灵魂，现在看来，连她那样优秀的人也没法幸免。
t_en: Well, they say that only love can tame a free spirit. It seems like even a carefree person like her cannot escape such a fate.

t_ch: 你怎么看待自己？
t_en: How do you see yourself?

t_ch: 我？呵…一事无成的累赘罢了。如果我父母的人生是一份完美答卷，那我就是答卷上唯一的污点。
t_en: Me? Heh... a useless burden. If you say my parents are leading a perfect life, then I'm probably the only flaw in their life.

t_ch: 不是有句话这么说吗：父母是真爱，孩子是意外。但我又偏偏不是他们亲生的……
t_en: Isn't there an old saying? If parents are true love, then children are just accidents. Then again, I'm not their biological child...

t_ch: 重组家庭？
t_en: A blended family?

t_ch: 不，那样至少能有一方血缘关系…我是养子。
t_en: No, that's if I'm related to one of them... I'm an adopted son.

t_ch: 难不成捡来的？
t_en: You were claimed from somewhere?

t_ch: 是啊，我是养子。
t_en: Yes, I'm their adopted son.

t_ch: 在我很小的时候，他们从公司旗下的孤儿院收养了我。毕竟单靠他们自己是无法生育的。
t_en: When I was a child, they adopted me from an IPC orphanage. After all, it's impossible for them to give birth to a child.

t_ch: 那时起我就对他们心生憧憬，希望有朝一日能回报他们的恩情。
t_en: From that day onward, I looked up to them, hoping that one day, I can repay their kindness.

t_ch: 可自从来到这里之后，我发现当初的豪言壮语实在太难达到了——他们是人中龙凤，哪怕没有我也能过得很好。反倒是我更需要他们的帮忙。
t_en: But ever since I've been here, I realized it was too difficult for me to live up to the grandiose promise I made at the beginning — they are the best of the best and can even get by each day without me. Instead, I am the one who needs their help.

t_ch: 唉，大恩难报啊……
t_en: *sigh* How can I ever repay their kindness...?

t_ch: …再见。
t_en: ...Bye.

t_ch: …嗯，再见。
t_en: ...Yeah, bye.

t_ch: …再见。
t_en: ...Bye.

t_ch: …嗯，再见。
t_en: ...Yeah, bye.

t_ch: 男人努力让表情显得平静。你知道，那只是一副脆弱的面具。
t_en: The man tries to stay calm. You can tell that's only a mask to hide his weakness.

t_ch: 又是你啊……
t_en: Oh, it's you again...

t_ch: 妈妈…爸爸……
t_en: Mom... Dad...

t_ch: 阴云重新覆盖了眼前的男人。他目光瞥向不远处的角落，仿佛在寻找可供躲藏的空间。
t_en: Dark clouds loom over the man before you. He glanced over to a corner not far away, as if looking for a space to hide.

t_ch: 抱歉，我…我还是找个凉快地方待着吧。
t_en: Sorry, I... I should look for a cool corner to rest.

t_ch: 男人努力让表情显得平静。你知道，那只是一副脆弱的面具。
t_en: The man tries to stay calm. You can tell that's only a mask to hide his weakness.

t_ch: 仔细想想，要是真能一辈子待在巢里，那也不赖。
t_en: Come to think of it. It would be a good thing if I could stay at home forever.

t_ch: 男人的脸上露出释怀的表情，好像在一瞬间接受了自己的现状。
t_en: The man reveals a look of relief. It's as if he has accepted his fate at that moment.

t_ch: 仔细想想，被这样的家庭收养，也是我的命运呢……
t_en: Come to think of it. I am probably destined to be adopted by such a family...

t_ch: 他们说要来匹诺康尼，我就跟着来；他们说要开店，我就帮着打杂。我是他们的儿子，也是最亲近的跟屁虫。
t_en: When they said they wanted to come to Penacony, I followed. When they said they wanted to open a store, I helped. I am their son and their closest tagger.

t_ch: 但这种感觉…并不坏。什么都不用操心，只需要照着指令去做就好。天底下再没有比这更轻松的生活了。
t_en: But this isn't... bad. I don't have to worry about anything; I just need to obey. There is no easier life than this.

t_ch: 或许我骨子里并不是一个喜欢独立的人。要是真能一辈子待在巢里，那也不赖。
t_en: Perhaps independence is not my thing. The idea of staying home forever doesn't sound bad to me.

t_ch: 哈哈，多少人想要这么厉害的父母都没处找去呢！
t_en: Haha, not everyone can have such amazing parents!

t_ch: 哈！之前我一定是脑子坏了才会抱怨个不停。
t_en: Ha! I must be crazy to grumble about my life.

t_ch: 眼下的生活有什么不好的吗？没有！多少人想要这么厉害的父母都没处找去呢！
t_en: What's so bad about my life? Nothing! Not everyone can have such amazing parents!

t_ch: 匹诺康尼真是个躺平的好地方。自从来到这里之后，好事情一件接着一件。
t_en: Penacony is such a wonderful place for one to chill and relax. Ever since I've been here, good things have been happening to me.

t_ch: …就比如这店里的飞碟堡，我每天都能畅吃到饱！
t_en: ...Such as the Floatdisc Burger from this shop. I can eat unlimited burgers till I'm full every day!

t_ch: 哈！之前我一定是脑子坏了才会抱怨个不停。
t_en: Ha! I must be crazy to grumble about my life.

t_ch: 眼下的生活有什么不好的吗？简直太好了！
t_en: What's so bad about my life? It's perfect!

t_ch: 我当初可真幸运——多少人想要这么厉害的父母都没处找去呢！
t_en: I was really fortunate — not everyone can have such amazing parents!

t_ch: 一个人类的父亲…怎么会是台老旧的机械呢？
t_en: How can the father of a human... be an old machine?

t_ch: 不行，再这么下去，我就要在这鬼地方看一辈子店了！
t_en: No way. If this goes on, I'll have to take care of this shop forever!

t_ch: 可是出去的话，我又能做什么呢…真见鬼，我怎么会变成这副废物模样！
t_en: But what can I do if I leave... Darn, why did I become such a good-for-nothing!?

t_ch: 一定是父亲把我保护得太好了。他以前就这样，恨不得把一切都装进瓶子里才行。
t_en: Dad must have been too protective of me. He's always like this. If possible, he would rather keep me safe in a bottle himself.

t_ch: 不对，它、它从来都不是我的父亲！它怎么会是我的父亲……
t_en: No. I—It's never my dad to begin with! How can he be my dad...

t_ch: 它是个机械啊！要是没有我每天帮忙上润滑油，它走两步道能摔三个跟头！
t_en: It's a machine! Its parts will drop from time to time without me helping him to apply lubricant every day!

t_ch: 曾经那么威风的家伙，如今却这么狼狈…智械的觉悟难道也像人类一样易碎吗？！
t_en: He was so fearsome in the past, and now he's looking pathetic... Could it be the consciousness of an Intellitron is as fragile as that of a human being!?

t_ch: 不行，再这么下去，我就要在这鬼地方看一辈子店了！
t_en: No way. If this goes on, I'll have to take care of this shop forever!

t_ch: 可是出去的话，我又能做什么呢…真见鬼，我怎么会变成这副废物模样！
t_en: But what can I do if I leave... Darn, why did I become such a good-for-nothing!?

t_ch: 一定是父亲把我保护得太好了。他以前就这样，恨不得把一切都装进瓶子里才行。
t_en: Dad must have been too protective of me. He's always like this. If possible, he would rather keep me safe in a bottle himself.

t_ch: 不对，它、它从来都不是我的父亲！它怎么会是我的父亲……
t_en: No. I—It's never my dad to begin with! How can he be my dad...

t_ch: 它是个机械啊！不再中用的机械……
t_en: It's a machine! A machine that has exceeded functional years...

t_ch: 这位智械仰望着天空，对周围的一切都不作反应，包括你。
t_en: The Intellitron gazes into the sky, not reacting to the surroundings, which include you.

t_ch: 即使经过梦境美化，你也能看出它的身躯有些破败、显然承受了太多岁月的消磨。
t_en: Even though the Dreamscape has been beautified, you can't help but notice how dilapidated its body is. It has endured years of wear and tear.

t_ch: 不管这副躯体曾经做出过怎样的壮举，人都是要服老的——智械也是。
t_en: No matter what formidable moves this body once managed, it is inevitable that everyone ages — including Intellitrons.

t_ch: 啊…真没想到，居然能在这里遇到其他人。你好。
t_en: Ah... I never imagined meeting other people out here. Hello.

t_ch: 你看上去不怎么惊讶。
t_en: You don't seem surprised.

t_ch: 我已经在梦境里待了很久了，能让我感到惊讶的事恐怕不多。
t_en: I have been in the Dreamscape for a long time. I'm afraid nothing surprises me anymore.

t_ch: 这里很危险。
t_en: It's very dangerous here.

t_ch: 危险？我倒没觉得。
t_en: Dangerous? I doubt so.

t_ch: 我已经在梦境里待了很久了。这里很安全…比现实要安全得多。
t_en: I've been here for a long time. It's very safe in here... safer than the reality.

t_ch: 比起之前哄闹的酒店环境，我更喜欢这样…一个人待着，只和我的画布、画笔在一起。
t_en: As compared to the former hotel from before, I prefer this place... Alone with my canvas and paintbrushes.

t_ch: 关于你的丈夫和女儿…
t_en: About your husband and daughter...
Stop —

t_ch: 在你提到她家人的一瞬间，夏洛特的人格骤然跳出了她的躯壳。
t_en: The moment you mention her family, Charlotte instantly broke out of her shell.

t_ch: …抱歉，我不想谈论这个。如你所见，我享受此刻的孤独。
t_en: ...Sorry, but I don't wish to talk about this. As you can see, I am enjoying my solitude.

t_ch: 能忍受孤独也不错。
t_en: If you can tolerate it, solitude isn't a bad idea.

t_ch: 忍受？不，你理解错了…我享受孤独。
t_en: Tolerate? No, you got me wrong... I enjoy solitary.

t_ch: 绘画就是我的生命。能自由地挥动画笔，为这个世界留下属于我的印记，这是赐福，也是……
t_en: Painting is my life. The freedom to brandish my paintbrush and leave my personal mark in this world is a blessing and a...

t_ch: …也是诅咒。一旦我的灵感枯竭…包裹我的就只剩痛苦。
t_en: ...and a curse. As soon as my creativity wilts... only pain remains.

t_ch: 小佩说的也许没错。我不会爱…我也没有资格去爱。
t_en: Perhaps Pennie was right. I don't know how to love... and I'm not qualified to love.

t_ch: 夏洛特的脸色黯淡了。但她的眼神中不含悔意，只剩纯净的痛苦。
t_en: Charlotte's mood darkens. There is no regret in her eyes, only pure pain.

t_ch: 想和你聊聊。
t_en: I'd like to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 我…不擅长聊天。但如果你坚持的话……
t_en: I... am not good with small talk, but if you insist...

t_ch: 你有什么得意的作品吗？
t_en: What's your favorite work of art?

t_ch: …得意？应该没有那样的作品。
t_en: ...My favorite? I don't think there is.

t_ch: 之于我，绘画是生命力的涌现和映射。简单如此。
t_en: For me, painting is an emergence and a mapping of life force. That's it.

t_ch: 和芸芸众生被缺陷填满的生命一样，我的画作里也尽是瑕疵。我的生命本不完整，自然也不可能交出完整的画作。
t_en: Like how lives are filled with imperfections, my paintings are filled with flaws. My life isn't complete, so naturally, I can never create a complete artwork.

t_ch: 但也有很多人，其中还不乏一些评论家，把「缺陷」视作美的一部分——我感激他们的赏识。但我笔下的作品到底表达了什么，映射了什么，我不指望他人真正理解。
t_en: But there are also many people, including some critics, who view "flaws" as beauty — I'm grateful for their appreciation. But what are my works trying to express? What are they portraying? I don't expect anyone to genuinely understand them.

t_ch: 非要说一幅相对满意的作品…《人间乐土》也许算吧。
t_en: If I must pick a favorite... then it's probably the Paradise on Earth.

t_ch: 你在乎名利多过家庭。
t_en: You care more about fame than family.

t_ch: 可能的确如此…了解我的背景的人大概都会这么觉得吧？
t_en: Maybe you're right... Anyone who knows my background would probably feel the same way, right?

t_ch: 连我自己也一度这么以为——我以为艺术家的名声就是我的终极追求，以为获得他人的认可就等于获得幸福。
t_en: Even I thought so myself — I thought an artist's reputation was my ultimate pursuit, and gaining the recognition of others was the same as achieving happiness.

t_ch: 我得到幸福了吗？也许有那么一阵…但存在我心里的那股暖流很快就流逝了。再后来，我还是宁愿把自己关在小屋子里，自顾自地画画。
t_en: But was I happy? Perhaps for a short moment... but that warmth in my heart disappeared shortly after. After that, I'd rather lock myself up in a small hut and paint alone.

t_ch: 后来我才终于明白，我从来不知道自己真正想要什么。我能做的只有一直画下去，直到梦境破碎，生命终结。
t_en: Later, I finally understood. I never knew what I truly wanted. All I can do now is to keep painting until the Dreamscape shatters and my life ends.

t_ch: 为什么要待在这里？
t_en: Why do you choose to stay here?

t_ch: 这里很安静，不会——至少暂时不会——被人打扰。
t_en: It's very quiet in here, and at least no one will disturb me — for now.

t_ch: 家族迟早会回来，酒店也总会回到原本熙攘的模样。这份平静迟早会被打破，美好的事物终有消失的一天。一切都是彼此的隐喻……
t_en: The Family will be back sooner or later, and the hotel will return to its busy state. This serenity will be broken some day, like how every good thing will disappear one day. Everything is a metaphor for another...

t_ch: …说了些不明所以的话，不好意思。我已经告诉你了，我不擅长聊天……
t_en: ...My apologies for saying such ridiculous words. As I told you, I'm bad with small talks...

t_ch: 不介意的话，我想继续画画了。
t_en: If you don't mind, I wish to continue painting.

t_ch: …再见。
t_en: ...Bye.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 我唯一想做的，就是在这个空无一人的梦境里安静地画画。
t_en: My only wish is to paint in this empty Dreamscape alone quietly.

t_ch: 请别告诉别人我在这里，好吗？
t_en: Please don't tell anyone I'm here, all right?

t_ch: 夏洛特周边的空气似乎被染上了颜色，化作一抹忧伤的紫。
t_en: The air surrounding Charlotte seems painted with colors, and it suddenly turns into a sad violet.

t_ch: 我一直有一个愿望…我想画出我心里的梦想之城，它真正该有的样子。
t_en: I always have a wish... I want to paint the city of my innermost dreams — the way it truly should be.

t_ch: 但我画不出来——我能绘制出远比一座城市更为抽象的事物，但只有这个题材，我无论如何都无法下笔。
t_en: But I can't — I've no issue painting something far more abstract than a city, but this is the only thing where I cannot put my ideas into action.

t_ch: 为了一场幻梦，我抛弃了所有现实。现在呢？我好像拥有了一切；我又好像一无所有。
t_en: For a dream, I abandon all reality. But what now? I seem to own everything, yet I seem to have nothing.

t_ch: 见到韦和小佩的时候…我很难用语言形容自己当时的心理活动。
t_en: When I saw Wes and Pennie... I found it difficult to describe my feelings at that time.

t_ch: 也许我会以《家人》为题，再画一幅画吧……
t_en: Maybe I will do another painting under the title, Kinship...

t_ch: 夏洛特周边的空气失去了颜色。
t_en: The air surrounding Charlotte loses color.

t_ch: 家人…到底意味着什么呢？
t_en: Kinship... what exactly does it mean?

t_ch: 有一件事是肯定的——我的确爱着韦和小佩。但我对他们的爱…好像和别人眼里的爱有很大区别。
t_en: One thing I'm sure of... I love Wes and Pennie. But my love for them... seems different from the love people see.

t_ch: 为什么爱一定要成为枷锁呢？为什么爱总要把人们捆绑在一起，逼迫他们互相依赖，一生一世都不能将视线从对方身上移开？
t_en: Why must love become a shackle? Why must love bundle people together, forcing them to depend on each other so that they can never take their eyes off each other for the rest of their lives?

t_ch: 见到韦和小佩的时候…我很难用语言形容自己当时的心理活动。我不想伤害他们，但我也理解不了他们眼中那种病态的爱。
t_en: When I saw Wes and Pennie... I found it difficult to describe my feelings at that time. I didn't want to hurt them, but I couldn't understand their toxic love too.

t_ch: 也许我会以《家人》为题，再画一幅画吧…我是不是不该和外人说这么多？
t_en: Maybe I will do another painting under the title, Kinship... Have I shared too much with an outsider?

t_ch: 请拿着这个吧，希望你能对我的秘密…尤其是我的家庭，守口如瓶。
t_en: Please, take this. I hope you can keep this a secret... Especially don't mention a word to my family.

t_ch: 夏洛特周边的空气失去了颜色。
t_en: The air surrounding Charlotte loses color.

t_ch: 家人…到底意味着什么呢？
t_en: Kinship... what exactly does it mean?

t_ch: 有一件事是肯定的——我的确爱着韦和小佩。但我对他们的爱…好像和别人眼里的爱有很大区别。
t_en: One thing I'm sure of... I love Wes and Pennie. But my love for them... seems different from the love people see.

t_ch: 为什么爱一定要成为枷锁呢？为什么爱总要把人们捆绑在一起，逼迫他们互相依赖，一生一世都不能将视线从对方身上移开？
t_en: Why must love become a shackle? Why must love bundle people together, forcing them to depend on each other so that they can never take their eyes off each other for the rest of their lives?

t_ch: 见到韦和小佩的时候…我很难用语言形容自己当时的心理活动。我不想伤害他们，但我也理解不了他们眼中那种病态的爱。
t_en: When I saw Wes and Pennie... I found it difficult to describe my feelings at that time. I didn't want to hurt them, but I couldn't understand their toxic love too.

t_ch: 也许我会以《家人》为题，再画一幅画吧……
t_en: Maybe I will do another painting under the title, Kinship...

t_ch: 绘画的过程总能令我沉醉，不管我在画什么。
t_en: The painting process always mesmerizes me, no matter what I'm painting.

t_ch: 每根线条都是一种超脱，每次落笔都能带我逃离这无趣的人生。
t_en: Every line drawn is a transcendence, and every time my brush touches the canvas, it takes me away from this uninteresting life.

t_ch: 夏洛特周边的空气似乎被染上了颜色，化作一抹欢脱的黄。
t_en: The air surrounding Charlotte seems painted with colors, and it suddenly turns into a carefree yellow.

t_ch: 你知道吗？我第一次画画是在两岁的时候…那时的我连一句完整的话都不会说。
t_en: Do you know? I started painting when I was two... I couldn't even complete a sentence then.

t_ch: 周围的孩子还在哭着闹着遍地打滚的时候，我就已经习惯了用绘画表达自己的内心世界。凡是能用画笔创作出来的，我都不想浪费言语去表达。
t_en: While other kids were wailing and rolling on the floor, I had already learned how to express my feelings through paintings. And if my artwork couldn't convey them, I didn't bother to say them either.

t_ch: 很早我就意识到了：能陪伴我走完这一生的不是我的亲人或朋友，而是这份与生俱来的天赋。
t_en: I realized it in my early stage of life that my family and friends aren't going to be the ones that accompanying me to the very end, but this talent of mine will.

t_ch: 每每陷入迷茫时，我总能在绘画中找到答案。你看上去…也像是个很有天分的人，虽然我不确定它是什么。
t_en: Every time I felt lost, I could find answers from painting. You seem... to be a very talented person, though I'm not sure what your talent is.

t_ch: 希望你能珍惜自己的天赋，做出一番不会令自己后悔的事业。收下这个吧——我不需要它了。
t_en: I hope you'll value your talent and use it to do something you'll not regret. Take this — I don't need it anymore.

t_ch: 夏洛特周边的空气似乎被染上了颜色，化作一抹欢脱的黄。
t_en: The air surrounding Charlotte seems painted with colors, and it suddenly turns into a carefree yellow.

t_ch: 你知道吗？我第一次画画是在两岁的时候…那时的我连一句完整的话都不会说。
t_en: Do you know? I started painting when I was two... I couldn't even complete a sentence then.

t_ch: 周围的孩子还在哭着闹着遍地打滚的时候，我就已经习惯了用绘画表达自己的内心世界。凡是能用画笔创作出来的，我都不想浪费言语去表达。
t_en: While other kids were wailing and rolling on the floor, I had already learned how to express my feelings through paintings. And if my artwork couldn't convey them, I didn't bother to say them either.

t_ch: 很早我就意识到了：能陪伴我走完这一生的不是我的亲人或朋友，而是这份与生俱来的天赋。
t_en: I realized it in my early stage of life that my family and friends aren't going to be the ones that accompanying me to the very end, but this talent of mine will.

t_ch: 每每陷入迷茫时，我总能在绘画中找到答案。你看上去…也像是个很有天分的人，虽然我不确定它是什么。
t_en: Every time I felt lost, I could find answers from painting. You seem... to be a very talented person, though I'm not sure what your talent is.

t_ch: 希望你能珍惜自己的天赋，做出一番不会令自己后悔的事业。
t_en: I hope you'll value your talent and use it to do something you'll not regret.

t_ch: 我不想再和所谓的评论家、鉴赏家打交道了——永远不想。
t_en: I don't wish to deal with the so-called critics and connoisseurs again — never.

t_ch: 夏洛特周边的空气似乎被染上了颜色，化作一抹愠怒的红。
t_en: The air surrounding Charlotte seems painted with colors, and it suddenly turns into an angry red.

t_ch: 我已经厌倦向他人解释我的作品了。
t_en: I'm sick and tired of explaining my artwork to others.

t_ch: 他们为什么总是执着于解读？假如我的画作里没有值得他们分析的东西…那是不是代表着它们就一文不值了？
t_en: Why are they always so obsessed with interpreting it? If there is nothing in my paintings worth analyzing... does that mean they are worthless?

t_ch: 我不想再和所谓的评论家、鉴赏家打交道了——永远不想。
t_en: I don't wish to deal with the so-called critics and connoisseurs again — never.

t_ch: 再见，陌生人。
t_en: Goodbye, stranger.

t_ch: 一只造型奇特的移动广告牌拦在你的必经之路上。
t_en: An unusual-looking mobile billboard blocks your path.

t_ch: 哦，这位{F#美丽的小姐}{M#英俊的先生}，欢迎来到匹诺康尼——恕我冒昧，请问您见过我的爱人吗？
t_en: Ah, {F#beautiful lady}{M#handsome gentleman}, welcome to Penacony! Excuse my boldness, but have you seen my love around here?

t_ch: 你谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我的身份并不重要，{F#小姐}{M#先生}！一位长得像广告牌的推销员，或者一只干着推销员工作的广告牌？
t_en: My identity is not important, {F#miss}{M#sir}! Am I a salesman that happens to resemble a billboard, or a billboard stuck in the role of a salesman?

t_ch: 不，我只是一个苦苦寻找梦中情人的{RUBY_B#钟情广告牌}可怜逐梦客{RUBY_E#}罢了。
t_en: I am neither. I am just a {RUBY_B#Lovestruck Billboard}poor Dreamchaser{RUBY_E#} tirelessly searching for the lover of my dreams.

t_ch: 在广告牌身上，原本应该印着商品的位置，你却只看到一张美丽的女子面孔。
t_en: On the billboard where a product should be placed, you can only see the face of a beautiful woman.

t_ch: 我的爱人，她是匹诺康尼的珍宝，人群中最耀眼的启明星，所有人都会被她吸去目光——没错，即使是广告牌也不例外！
t_en: My love, she is the treasure of Penacony, the brightest star in the crowd, catching everyone's attention — That's right, not even a billboard is exempt!

t_ch: 啊…她是如此令人过目不忘，可我已经很久没见到她了……
t_en: Ah... She is unforgettable, but I haven't seen her for such a long time...

t_ch: 广告牌上的画面随着它的语气变暗了，看起来居然有些可怜。
t_en: The screen of the billboard grows dim as its tone changes. You actually start to feel a little pity.

t_ch: 我也没见过。
t_en: I haven't seen her.

t_ch: 好吧…看来我还要继续等下去……
t_en: Alright... I guess I'll have to keep searching...

t_ch: 如果您以后在某处遇见她，请务必替我递上一句话：「有只广告牌在中央车站前等你，它会一直等下去的！」
t_en: If you happen to meet her somewhere, please tell her one thing for me: "There is a billboard in front of the Golden Central Station waiting for you. It will always be waiting for you!"

t_ch: 我会帮你留意。
t_en: I will let her know.

t_ch: 太好了，{F#小姐}{M#先生}——您真是个大好人！
t_en: Thank you, {F#miss}{M#sir}... You truly are a kind person!

t_ch: 如果您以后在某处遇见她，请务必替我递上一句话：「有只广告牌在中央车站前等你，它会一直等下去的！」
t_en: If you happen to meet her somewhere, please tell her one thing for me: "There is a billboard in front of the Golden Central Station waiting for you. It will always be waiting for you!"

t_ch: 没见过。
t_en: ...Didn't see her.

t_ch: 那真是太遗憾了！她是匹诺康尼的珍宝，人群中最耀眼的启明星，所有人都会被她吸去目光——即使是广告牌也不例外！
t_en: That is a true pity! She is the treasure of Penacony, the brightest star in the crowd, catching everyone's attention — That's right, not even a billboard is exempt!

t_ch: 啊…她是如此令人过目不忘，等您见过她就明白了！
t_en: Ah... She is unforgettable, and you will understand when you see her for yourself!

t_ch: 广告牌开心地晃了晃。画面上的女子确实气质独特，很有魅力。
t_en: The billboard sways happily. The young girl on the screen does indeed seem to have a unique quality. She is very charming.

t_ch: 我会帮你留意。
t_en: I will let her know.

t_ch: 太好了，{F#小姐}{M#先生}——您真是个大好人！
t_en: Thank you, {F#miss}{M#sir}... You truly are a kind person!

t_ch: 如果您以后在某处遇见她，请务必替我递上一句话：「有只广告牌在中央车站前等你，它会一直等下去的！」
t_en: If you happen to meet her somewhere, please tell her one thing for me: "There is a billboard in front of the Golden Central Station waiting for you. It will always be waiting for you!"

t_ch: 这是什么广告？
t_en: What is this advert for?

t_ch: 哦不不不，这可不是广告。
t_en: Oh, no no! This is not an advertisement!

t_ch: 我正在寻找我的爱人！她是匹诺康尼的珍宝，人群中最耀眼的启明星，所有人都会被她吸去目光——即使是广告牌也不例外！
t_en: This is my love that I am searching for! She is the treasure of Penacony, the brightest star in the crowd, catching everyone's attention — That's right, not even a billboard is exempt!

t_ch: 啊…她是如此令人过目不忘，您对这张脸有印象吗？
t_en: Ah... She is unforgettable. I don't suppose you recognize this face, do you?
